situation: audit-2026-07-08-046 finding SP-SCR-H-002 (high): xhs_keepalive.py update_env_cookie uses ENV_FILE.write_text() non-atomically. Concurrent cron invocations can observe a truncated/partially-written .env, corrupting credentials. Derived fix from deep audit cycle.
action: Replace direct write_text with tempfile.mkstemp + os.fdopen + fsync + os.replace pattern in same directory. Ensures readers always see either old or new complete content; tmp file cleaned up on any failure including KeyboardInterrupt. Added import os, tempfile at module top.
outcome_evidence: opt-worktree.sh cmd_commit output: '✓ 提交到 opt-scripts-1783396445 方向=scripts:xhs-keepalive-atomic'; branch auto/opt-scripts-1783396445 pushed to origin (verified in push output). Main worktree file restored by cp-flow as expected.
next_suggestion: Continue dispatching audit-2026-07-08-046 pending route-fixes. Next high-severity candidate: SP-SRV-M-002 (routes.js cache thundering herd) or SP-SRV-M-005 (routes.js 502 err.message leak). After all pending cleared, status flips to cycle-complete → triggers next full audit.

audit_findings.finding: ENV_FILE.write_text(new_content) is non-atomic. Concurrent cron executions of xhs_keepalive.py can race: process A opens .env for write (truncates), process B reads mid-write and gets partial/corrupt content. Credential truncation risk under concurrent scheduling.
audit_findings.remediation: Use tempfile.mkstemp in same dir + os.replace for atomic rename. fsync before rename to ensure durability. Cleanup tmp on any exception.
